claim_text: Train 12040 currently runs daily from NDLS 06:20 to KGM 11:55 with the recorded nine-station sequence.
For Marks 23 kg checked bag, the current IndiGo domestic baseline creates excess-baggage cost above a 15 kg allowance; the current 10 kg prepaid slab is INR 6500.
notes: Fare bundle may change; live quote required.

claim_text: Bhowali is materially closer by road to Kathgodam than to Pantnagar under the state tourism access description, supporting KGM as practical rail arrival.
notes: Exact winter drive time remains open.

claim_text: The 12040 rail option is the preferred working arrival because it is cheaper with 23 kg baggage and currently reaches the first base earlier in the day than the midday DEL-PGH pattern.
notes: Inference from current schedules and cost components; not a booking fact.

claim_text: Bhowali is the preferred first base for the Kainchi/Bhumiyadhar and Nainital-side formal locations before moving east to Almora.
The proposed internal order accounts for all sixteen formal Kumaon A locations without altering any A/B/C status.
notes: Route order is a BRONS working proposal for ZILVER verification.

claim_text: A five-night Almora/Kasar block can cover the Almora circuit, Kasar ridge and Jageshwar while retaining one weather/access buffer.
notes: Exact lodging side and road times remain open.

claim_text: Babaji Cave requires a distinct full day because the official YSS page places it 25 km beyond Dwarahat, reports about a one-hour climb and six-to-eight hours for the ashram-cave return.
notes: Winter trail and daylight check mandatory.

claim_text: Bodh Ashram remains formally A but cannot be treated as a reliable visit slot until physical identity, management and access are confirmed.
Haldwani is the preferred final Kumaon base because it supports the Haidakhan day and direct boarding of 12092 without an uphill return to Kathgodam.
notes: Local road/taxi must be live checked.

claim_text: Train 12092 currently provides a direct Sunday 3 January 2027 working exit from HDW 05:40 to HW 11:05.
notes: Service-day details cross-checked; misleading all-days header rejected; live official recheck mandatory.

claim_text: Haridwar/Kankhal is the best next existing projectcluster because the direct 12092 arrives before noon and avoids bus, flight and plains taxi.
notes: Route decision, not a new place valuation.

claim_text: The current 18478 schedule provides a daily daytime bridge from HW 06:54 to MTJ 13:45 with all segment stops recorded.
notes: High delay signal and future diversion require buffer/live recheck.

claim_text: The provisional remainder Haridwar/Kankhal -> Braj/Agra -> Varanasi -> Bodh Gaya -> Kolkata/Dakshineswar is geographically and operationally stronger than jumping directly from Kumaon to Kolkata.
notes: No content research of successor clusters performed.

claim_text: The direct 13020 KGM-HWH option is not preferred because its current duration is about 37h25 and it skips the desired intermediate cluster sequence.
All recommended train-stop instances are preserved as grey transport markers with one train-logo style and never acquire formal or advisory A/B/C status.
notes: Coordinates remain a blocking final-KML recheck where not already permanent.
